criterion_selection.question: To accurately diagnose complete right bundle branch block, which of the following diagnostic criteria should be evaluated?
options: Presence of an RSR' pattern in leads V1 and V2
Presence of low voltage QRS complexes in precordial leads
Presence of pathological Q waves in leads V1 and V2
Presence of ST elevation in leads V1 and V2
Presence of flattened T waves in inferior leads
criterion_selection.answer: Presence of an RSR' pattern in leads V1 and V2

finding.question: Regarding the criterion you selected, does this ECG show an RSR' pattern in lead V1 or V2, where there is an additional positive deflection (r' or R') following the initial r or R wave?

decision.question: Based on the finding identified above, does this patient have complete right bundle branch block?
options: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis
decision.answer: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis

criterion_selection.question: Since the typical RSR' pattern was not observed, which alternative diagnostic criterion should be evaluated to diagnose complete right bundle branch block?
options: Presence of a broad, notched R wave in lead V6
Presence of a broad monophasic R wave in lead V1
Presence of significant ST depression in lead V1
Presence of a deep, wide S wave in lead V1
criterion_selection.answer: Presence of a broad monophasic R wave in lead V1

finding.question: Regarding the criterion you selected, does this ECG show broad monophasic R waves in lead V1? Note that a broad monophasic R wave is defined as a positive R wave with a duration greater than 120 milliseconds and a R wave peak time greater than 55 milliseconds.

grounding.question: Within the lead V1, in which of the following segments can you observe the QRS complexes with broad monophasic R waves? The options below refer to time ranges on the ECG signal, provided in seconds.

decision.question: Based on all the findings identified so far, does this patient have complete right bundle branch block?
options: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis
decision.answer: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis

criterion_selection.question: In addition to the finding in lead V1 or V2, which other diagnostic criterion should be evaluated to diagnose complete right bundle branch block?
options: Presence of flattened T waves in inferior leads
Presence of a slurred S wave in lateral leads
Absence of any S waves in leads I and V6
Presence of pathological Q waves in lateral leads
Presence of prominent U waves in lateral leads
criterion_selection.answer: Presence of a slurred S wave in lateral leads

finding.question: Regarding the criterion you selected, does this ECG show slurred S waves in at least two of the lateral leads, including leads I, aVL, V5, and V6? Note that a slurred S wave is defined as a S wave with a duration greater than that of the preceding R wave or greater than 40 milliseconds when there is a preceding R wave.

grounding.question: Which of the following leads show the slurred S waves? Select all possible leads from the options below.
Within the selected leads, in which of the following segments can you observe the QRS complexes with slurred S waves? The options below refer to time ranges on the ECG signal, provided in seconds.

decision.question: Based on all the findings identified so far, does this patient have complete right bundle branch block?
options: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis
decision.answer: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis

criterion_selection.question: In addition to the findings you have identified so far, which other diagnostic criterion should be evaluated to diagnose complete right bundle branch block?
options: Presence of pathological Q waves in inferior leads
Variable QRS duration across different beats
Reduction in QRS amplitude in limb leads

finding.question: Regarding the criterion you selected, is the QRS duration prolonged on this ECG? Note that a QRS duration is considered to be prolonged if it is greater than 120 milliseconds.

grounding.question: In which of the following segments can you observe a QRS wave that demonstrates the prolonged QRS duration? The options below refer to time ranges on the ECG signal, provided in seconds.
For the selected segment, which range does the measured QRS duration fall into?

decision.question: Based on all the findings identified so far, does this patient have complete right bundle branch block?
options: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis
